response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a strong full-stack web developer with deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise, but she fundamentally misses every mandatory requirement for this role. Her C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project using C++ for audio synthesis — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth, no financial domain knowledge (capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking), and no exposure to Summit, Kafka/MQ, or Python. The RIDE Capital role involved investment-adjacent work but used Node.js/TypeScript exclusively. This resume represents a different engineering profile entirely.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience across multiple roles, including GitLab, Docker, and artifact management
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via public conference talks and multi-day workshops
Broad polyglot background (DLang, Java, C#, JavaScript) showing adaptability across paradigms
concerns: C++ experience is minimal — roughly 6 months of relevant work plus a thesis project, versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero financial domain exposure: no capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or related systems anywhere in the history
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React) is entirely misaligned with the role's Windows C++ / SQL Server / Oracle requirements
